ops.body:
Investigated (subagent). The prescribed fix is WRONG and would break CI — do not apply MCP-form patterns.

Root cause is NOT a wholesale CLI→MCP rewording. The warn messages are CONDITIONAL on mcp_active(evt) (plugin/hooks/common.py:270): MCP-form when a cc-notes MCP liveness marker sits under the repo git dir, CLI-form otherwise. The inline tests/{} rows have ONE static pattern each. The OLD patterns (--attach / cc-notes log add / cc-notes task add) match the CLI-form message, which is what a NON-MCP run produces — and cc-notes CI's hook-tests job runs `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` on a fresh checkout with NO cc-notes MCP → mcp_active False → CLI-form → OLD patterns PASS. That is why the drift shipped in v0.27.0 and v0.28.0. The 5 failures only appear in an MCP-active DEV shell (cc-context/cc-notes with the MCP running). Swapping to MCP-form patterns just moves the failure: it passes in dev but BREAKS CI (empirically confirmed in an isolated non-MCP run — "doesn't match 'attach param'").

Correct fix = MODE-AGNOSTIC patterns present in BOTH branches:
- record.py:414 (cp -R) → "attach"  (or "cc-notes log entry" from the unconditional lede)
- record.py:415 (mv crash) → "cc-notes log entry"  (unconditional lede line ~453)
- record.py:417 (Write log) → "attach"
- record.py:547 (ephemeral) → "stores an artifact"  (or "attach")
- record.py:757 (plan) → "outlives the session"  (shared by PLAN_TEACH and PLAN_TEACH_MCP, record.py:674/690)
Precedent: record.py:632 already uses Warn(pattern="attach"), a mode-agnostic row that passes in both modes.

Two landing blockers: (1) cc-notes @ is a concurrent session's 8430-line WIP; my edit got swept into their rewire commit and was deliberately dropped in "fix(hooks): drop record.py test-expectation WIP swept into the rewire commit" (4424c703, pushed to main). (2) cc-context packs.toml pin @latest resolves to the newest RELEASE TAG (v0.28.0=a231e77c), not the branch tip — both released tags carry the stale patterns, so `pack update` alone keeps the 5 failures; reaching 312/312 in cc-context needs a NEW cc-notes release containing the fix. Left in_progress for orchestrator to redirect.
